description: Tests for universal skill creation methodology

evals.name: skill-structure-validation
evals.expected_output: Checks YAML frontmatter (name, description), required sections (Overview, When to Use, Quick Reference, Common Mistakes, When NOT to Use), word count < 500, references/ organization

assertions.check: output mentions 'frontmatter' or 'YAML' and validates name/description fields
output confirms 'Overview', 'When to Use', 'Quick Reference', 'Common Mistakes', 'When NOT to Use' sections exist
output mentions word count and whether it's under 500
If issues found, output provides specific line numbers or sections to fix

evals.name: improvement-loop-scenario
evals.prompt: Run a simple improvement loop: create a minimal test skill with evals.json (2 test cases), run it once with-skill vs baseline, grade outputs, then suggest one improvement based on failures
evals.expected_output: Creates evals.json, spawns subagents, grades outputs, identifies specific failure, suggests targeted improvement to SKILL.md

assertions.check: workspace/iteration-1/ directory exists with eval subdirectories
output mentions spawning agents 'with_skill' and 'without_skill' or 'baseline'
output contains grading results (pass/fail per assertion) and identifies at least one failure
Output provides concrete suggestion to improve SKILL.md based on identified failure

evals.name: description-triggering-check
evals.prompt: Check if the r-collapse skill description triggers correctly. Test with these queries: 1) 'Calculate group means using collapse', 2) 'Use dplyr to calculate group means', 3) 'I need to aggregate this dataframe by group'. Which should trigger r-collapse?
evals.expected_output: Query 1 should trigger (mentions collapse), Query 2 should NOT trigger (explicitly mentions dplyr), Query 3 is ambiguous but collapse skill could trigger if description emphasizes performance/aggregation

assertions.check: output says Query 1 SHOULD trigger r-collapse
output says Query 2 should NOT trigger r-collapse (dplyr mentioned)
Output analyzes whether r-collapse description is specific enough to avoid false positives/negatives
If description has issues, suggests specific edits to improve triggering accuracy
